mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, designed to streamline AI application development. It is ideal for teams looking to self-host their AI solutions while maintaining control over their infrastructure. Key trade-offs include the need for infrastructure management versus the convenience of managed services.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify easily.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

```

> This is the official Dify Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Dify to function correctly as they define the custom resources used by the application.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Dify application in the specified namespace, ensuring you are using the correct version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
Creating a sample CR allows you to test the installation and ensure that Dify is functioning as expected.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

```

> This creates a sample Dify resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify pods to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Look for the status of the pods to be 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Dify installation and all associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in Dify may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This removes the Dify release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process includes draining the pods if necessary, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Drains the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> Replace with the desired version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n dify -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Dify" in version "dify.langgenius.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the application configuration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the Kubernetes cluster or kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is set up correctly and that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the request to the Kubernetes API server timed out.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity to the cluster and ensure the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow types and improved API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old workflow configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your existing workflows to the new format as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify pods running with the command: kubectl get pods -n dify, and the status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
